wheels: Hi, have a nice game
NicelyManifest: You are happy that we both play fairly slowly?
C[NicelyManifest: Few seem to liek split corner fuseki
C[NicelyManifest: You do not chat in games?
C[wheels: Not too often, but no problems with chatting
C[wheels: I'm always lost in the opening
C[wheels: The more I hear about it the worse it get's
C[NicelyManifest: I find it immensely useful to  watch pro game videos to get a whole board feel. Have done most days for 3 years now
wheels: The joseki was set after you reacted, finisheing it is not rushing
NicelyManifest: It is rushing it. You do not pause to consider teunki
NicelyManifest: Play fast and it is hard to consider them
C[NicelyManifest: I talk to much I guess. Say so if you prefer quiet
C[NicelyManifest: My key point is to challenge habit as teh key way to improve
NicelyManifest: D13 instead of D14, for example
wheels: As fro top left: when I played d15 I stated I want d10 too.
NicelyManifest: An you can leave C14 and D15 alone
NicelyManifest: Just suggesting a concept that has helped me
NicelyManifest: I have never played E3 before, for example
wheels: My response was fixed for me. But sure there other possibilities.
NicelyManifest: I say what I say to help not criticise
C[wheels: Or offence? English is not my native language ...
C[NicelyManifest: The manager of my team Liverpool is a most wonderful German ...
C[wheels: Klopp? Everyone was mad here when he left to England
C[NicelyManifest: I gully understand. He is the most remarkable human being I have ever witnessed
NicelyManifest: All that ability yet small ego
wheels: But he is still very prominent here, because he's busy doing insurance advertisments
C[NicelyManifest: Dortmund were successful under him - eventually
NicelyManifest: I have deviated from joseki to try to illustrate how different Go can be when we take new paths
C[wheels: Oh, I know it's very different, but for me it usually does not work out well to leave the few joseki paths I know
NicelyManifest: I understand, but you may not be giving enough time
NicelyManifest: I will try to match your pace
C[wheels: I really do rush in all of my games, it's nice to play slowly once in a while
C[NicelyManifest: You will find that your play level goes up to about 2k
NicelyManifest: 1. Narrows vision of the whole board
NicelyManifest: 2. Denies exploration beyond the obvious/habit
C[NicelyManifest: And I find slow much more relaxing
C[NicelyManifest: That same calm we get when we watch someone else play - and the same rise in play understand from that detachment of emotion
C[wheels: Yes, butstill I tend to play fast as soon as my oppnent does, no matter what time settings we agreed upon.
C[NicelyManifest: You can learn to overcome that - especially when you start to see how slow many of their moves are
C[NicelyManifest: Key is mindfulness - you know about this?
C[wheels: Well, intellectually yes, but still I make so many obvious mistakes but just not reading
NicelyManifest: When I calm my mind and I see that I have made a mistake I stay calm. I then reasses and move on, often miking my loss
NicelyManifest: Otherwise, I would then be emotionally irrational
C[wheels: This was played a little greedily by white
NicelyManifest: I was going to say by Black!
NicelyManifest: Overstretching is a habit that plagues me still
wheels: The invasion was very deep I think
C[NicelyManifest: I like to test my reading skills
C[NicelyManifest: My attack strengthend you - well played
C[NicelyManifest: But stretching reading is vital
C[NicelyManifest: You play the right end moves
C[NicelyManifest: end game is much better delineated
C[wheels: That was a big loss for black
C[NicelyManifest: You play the end game like a dan player
C[NicelyManifest: You get the priorities right
NicelyManifest: Thanks for a great game
wheels: I was sure I won though :)
NicelyManifest: You played better than I
NicelyManifest: How did it feel to play slow and sharp?
wheels: OK, got to leave, see you around!
